entities.name: FOOD LEGUMES
entities.description: Leguminous plants used for human consumption, crucial for societal challenges.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION
entities.description: Efforts to reduce or prevent climate change impacts.

entities.name: AGROBIODIVERSITY CONSERVATION
entities.description: The preservation of biological diversity within agricultural systems.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming practices that meet current needs without compromising future generations.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: Reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: HUMAN HEALTH
entities.description: The overall state of physical and mental well-being of humans.

entities.name: PLANT-BASED DIETS
entities.description: Diets consisting primarily or entirely of foods derived from plants.

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum, a food legume targeted by the INCREASE project.

entities.name: COMMON BEAN
entities.description: Phaseolus vulgaris, a food legume targeted by the INCREASE project.

entities.name: LENTIL
entities.description: Lens culinaris, a food legume targeted by the INCREASE project.

entities.name: LUPIN
entities.description: Lupinus albus and L. mutabilis, food legumes targeted by the INCREASE project.

entities.name: INTELLIGENT COLLECTIONS
entities.description: A new approach to conserve, manage, and characterize genetic resources using nested core collections.

entities.name: NESTED CORE COLLECTIONS
entities.description: Collections of genetic resources composed of purified accessions.

entities.name: SINGLE-SEED DESCENT-PURIFIED ACCESSIONS
entities.description: A method to purify genetic lines to create inbred lines.

entities.name: INBRED LINES
entities.description: Genetically uniform lines resulting from self-pollination or purification.

entities.name: GENEBANKS
entities.description: Repositories that store and maintain genetic material.

entities.name: ON-FARM
entities.description: Agricultural settings where crops are grown by farmers.

entities.name: GENOTYPIC CHARACTERIZATION
entities.description: The process of identifying the genetic makeup of an organism.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC CHARACTERIZATION
entities.description: The process of observing and measuring physical traits of an organism.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of measuring phenotypic traits.

entities.name: GENE DISCOVERY
entities.description: The process of identifying genes associated with specific traits.

entities.name: METABOLOMICS
entities.description: The large-scale study of small molecules, known as metabolites.

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTOMICS
entities.description: The study of the transcriptome—the complete set of RNA transcripts.

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
entities.description: Computational systems capable of performing tasks that typically require human intelligence.

entities.name: SMART TOOLS
entities.description: Advanced technological tools used for agricultural analysis and management.

entities.name: CITIZEN SCIENCE EXPERIMENT
entities.description: Research conducted with the participation of the general public.

entities.name: DECENTRALIZED DATA MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A system of managing data across multiple nodes rather than a central authority.

entities.name: DYNAMIC CONSERVATION
entities.description: An innovative system for the conservation and use of genetic resources.

entities.name: QUALITY
entities.description: The characteristics of the crop that determine its value or suitability.

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of a crop to adjust to its environment.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area.

entities.name: INCREASE
entities.description: The project focused on Intelligent Collections of food-legume genetic resources.
